abstract: Efficient adaptation of Large Language Models (LLMs) to specialized downstream tasks is often hindered by the substantial computational resources required and the technical complexity of implementing diverse fine-tuning methodologies. We introduce LLAMAFACTORY, an open-source, unified framework designed to democratize and streamline the efficient fine-tuning of over 100 language models. The framework integrates a comprehensive suite of cutting-edge optimization techniques—such as LoRA, GaLore, and DoRA—with various training objectives, including supervised fine-tuning (SFT), direct preference optimization (DPO), and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). A key feature of the framework is LLAMABOARD, a zero-code web interface that allows users to configure experiments, monitor real-time training dynamics, and evaluate model performance without programming. Our empirical evaluations demonstrate that LLAMAFACTORY significantly reduces memory overhead while maintaining high throughput and performance across language modeling and text generation benchmarks. Since its release, the framework has gained significant traction in the research community, serving as a robust foundation for diverse LLM applications and studies.
